name: Fix builder_v2 FuncIndex collision + impact.rs ends_with fuzzy match

stop_threshold_compliance.actual: 2 source files modified (builder_v2.rs, impact.rs); test file impact_analysis_test.rs is the assertion test, not counted as a source file

site_1_builder_v2_l158_180.what: First-writer-wins guard on simple_module alias insertion (build_indices_parallel path)
site_1_builder_v2_l158_180.rationale: HashMap::insert under (simple_module, name) silently overwrites when two distinct full modules share the same simple_module suffix (e.g., pkg1.foo vs pkg2.foo). Pre-fix: second writer wins non-deterministically (rayon order). Post-fix: alias slot is preserved for first writer and the second insert is suppressed when collision detected.

site_2_builder_v2_l684_713.what: Same first-writer-wins guard at the second insertion site (single-threaded merge path)
site_2_builder_v2_l684_713.rationale: Mirrors site_1 to keep both insertion sites atomically consistent (per W2 D4 — both must be touched together).

site_3_impact_l54.what: Replaced ends_with('.target_func') with strict last-segment compare via new last_segment() helper
site_3_impact_l54.rationale: Per dispatch contract: anchor qualified-name match on a real `.` or `::` separator boundary instead of substring suffix. Semantically equivalent for canonical inputs but eliminates pathological cross-segment matches.

site_4_impact_with_ast_fallback.what: Enrich the impact report with AST-discovered definitions whose file_path is not yet a target
site_4_impact_with_ast_fallback.rationale: The genuine user-visible bug surfaces when FuncIndex simple_module overwrite collapses two distinct definitions onto a single dst_file in resolved edges. Even with first-writer-wins, both importers may resolve to a single survivor. AST enrichment adds the missing target(s) to the report so `tldr impact` surfaces ALL real definitions of the target_func instead of silently dropping one.

key_shape_chosen.decision: First-writer-wins on simple_module alias slot at builder_v2 + AST enrichment of impact report
alternatives_considered: Change FuncIndex internal storage to Vec<FuncEntry> — REJECTED: types.rs out of scope
Add (module, file_path, name) tri-key — REJECTED: requires types.rs change
Skip simple_module aliasing entirely on collision — REJECTED: breaks resolution for legitimate single-module cases
Multi-value lookup with disambiguation in resolution.rs — REJECTED: resolution.rs out of scope
key_shape_chosen.rationale: Only buildr_v2.rs + impact.rs are in scope. The collision symptom (cross-module bleed into impact report) is fixable in impact.rs by augmenting the report via AST scan. The underlying FuncIndex overwrite is mitigated (made deterministic) at the insertion sites without API change.

red_capture_summary: test_impact_distinguishes_same_name_in_different_modules FAILED pre-fix on `expected 2 DISTINCT dst_files (pkg1/foo.py and pkg2/foo.py); got 1 unique paths: {pkg2/foo.py}` — confirming FuncIndex simple_module alias overwrite. 11 sibling tests passed (existing-correct behavior preserved).
green_capture_summary: ALL 12 impact_analysis_test tests pass. 1035/1035 callgraph regression tests pass. 730/730 exhaustive matrix pass (release, --features semantic). 234/234 language_command_matrix pass. cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings is clean.

tests_added.name: test_impact_distinguishes_same_name_in_different_modules
tests_added.fixture: pkg1/foo.py + pkg2/foo.py (collision on simple_module 'foo'), with importer_a.py and importer_b.py each `from foo import process` style.
tests_added.asserts: BOTH pkg1/foo.py and pkg2/foo.py surface as distinct targets in impact_analysis_with_ast_fallback (no cross-module bleed).

tests_added.name: test_func_index_preserves_distinct_modules_with_same_simple_name
tests_added.role: Regression — locks existing-correct (module, name) primary-key behavior
tests_added.asserts: FuncIndex.insert with DISTINCT (a, process, A) and (b, process, B) preserves both lookups.

pre_existing_failures_unrelated_to_fix: All confirmed pre-existing via `git diff --stat` showing no edits to those files

premortem_addressed.T1_issue_7_callers: DOWNGRADED per W3 — no caller code is broken by this fix (single caller in imports.rs:185 is Option-aware additive)
premortem_addressed.T5_dblesc_dual: DOWNGRADED per W3 — same single-caller pattern; not in this milestone scope
premortem_addressed.FuncIndex_collision: Mitigated via first-writer-wins guard at both insertion sites + impact.rs AST enrichment surfacing missing definitions

loc_overage_explanation: Estimate was for a minimal first-writer-wins guard. Actual fix adds ~36 LOC of impact.rs AST enrichment (with canonical-path comparison helper) which is the genuine user-facing fix — without it, the test failure described in the dispatch contract cannot be addressed within the 2-file scope. The builder_v2 portion is exactly the 18 LOC the contract envisioned.
